title: Syzygium valetonianum
taxonomy: Syzygium valetonianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is a type of evergreen tree native to the island of New Guinea. This species is part of the Syzygium genus, which includes many other species of plants commonly known as lillypilly or waterberry. The classification of Syzygium valetonianum places it within the order Myrtales and the clade Rosids. It is closely related to other members of the Myrtaceae family, which includes plants like eucalyptus and guava.
uses: Syzygium valetonianum, a plant species found in Indonesia, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties.
